title: Apple plans to change its Hide My Email privacy feature that could make it less effective
blurb: Apple will move Hide My Email's anonymized addresses from @icloud.com to a distinct @private.icloud.com domain in the coming weeks, letting apps and websites identify and block the masked sign-ups the feature was designed to obscure. Existing addresses keep forwarding, but developers must update mail filtering to avoid dropping messages, and the change lands months after Apple disclosed a Hide My Email user's real identity under subpoena.

title: Rockwell Automation Patches Vulnerabilities in ICS Controllers and Software
blurb: Rockwell Automation released patches for multiple high- and critical-severity flaws across its Logix and CompactLogix controllers, Flex I/O Ethernet/IP adapters, RSLinx, and FactoryTalk suite, including authentication bypass, improper API authorization, and several non-recoverable DoS faults. None of the newly disclosed bugs are under active exploitation, but the critical Flex I/O flaw lets an unauthenticated attacker change a device's web password, so OT teams running edge-exposed adapters should prioritize patching.

title: iRhythm Confirms Data Stolen in Hack
blurb: iRhythm, maker of the Zio wearable ECG monitor, disclosed in a Monday SEC filing that a social-engineering attack on a third-party-hosted business application led to the theft of data, with a threat actor claiming on June 9 to hold proprietary data and patient protected health information. The company says clinical and medical-device systems were unaffected but has not confirmed the actor's claims or the scope, and no known extortion group has taken credit.

title: UK Social Media Ban for Minors Has Privacy Experts Worried
blurb: The UK will ban under-16s from user-to-user social platforms like Instagram, TikTok, and X starting in early 2027, with possible limits on infinite scrolling and digital curfews for under-18s. Privacy experts warn the mandate could push platforms toward intrusive age-verification that normalizes identity collection and surveillance, mirroring bans already in force in Australia and proposed in Canada and several EU states.

title: Apple collects every tap to deliver App Store personalized recommendations
blurb: Security researchers at Mysk found that Apple's App Store logs every tap users make to power its new Personalized Collections feature, capturing identifiable analytics that can even derive a user's typing speed during searches, with no setting to disable the collection. The feature began rolling out this week in the U.S., and Apple currently offers no opt-out toggle for the underlying data capture.

title: What’s new in Databricks Platform security and compliance at Data + AI Summit 2026
blurb: Databricks announced new platform security capabilities at Data + AI Summit 2026, including general availability of Automatic Identity Management for Microsoft Entra ID on AWS and GCP, AIM for Okta in public preview, and Context-Based Ingress policies. The release also adds Private Network Gateway and expanded Private Link support for Lakebase, plus broader AWS GovCloud coverage for AI services and upcoming FedRAMP High support on Azure Commercial.

title: AI’s constant patching treadmill can be a security problem
blurb: Backslash Security reviewed two months of Claude Code update logs and found Anthropic silently shipped 30-plus security-relevant patches between April and early June 2026, covering prompt injection, data poisoning, OAuth credential leakage, and arbitrary code execution. One fix closed a bypass where adding a single backslash defeated guardrails against catastrophic deletion commands. The takeaway: AI coding tools patch at a far higher cadence than traditional software, so enable auto-updates and track changelogs for undisclosed fixes.

title: Twenty, which uses AI to help US military hackers penetrate adversary computer networks, raised a $100M Series B led by Accel at a $1B valuation (Colin Demarest/Axios)
blurb: Twenty raised a $100M Series B led by Accel at a $1B valuation to scale its AI platform that helps US military hackers penetrate adversary computer networks. The round signals continued investor appetite for offensive-cyber tooling built directly for government use, joining a growing cohort of defense-tech startups selling AI capabilities to the Pentagon and intelligence agencies.

title: The Chainguard Athena coalition already shipped 2,000 patches across 500 open source projects
blurb: Chainguard launched Athena, an industry coalition of more than two dozen members including BNY, Cisco, Cloudflare, Docker, and JPMorganChase, that pools pre-disclosure open source vulnerability findings and remediates them under embargo. The group says it has processed over 20,000 findings and shipped 2,000 patches across 500 projects, many sourced from frontier-AI bug-hunting programs like Anthropic's Project Glasswing and OpenAI's Daybreak.

title: India's Telegram ban hit the UAE too. Here's how to get around it
blurb: India banned Telegram nationwide until June 22 under Section 69A of the IT Act, but the block spilled across borders after Reliance's AS18101 began announcing Telegram's BGP prefixes, dropping access for users as far as the UAE. Kentik's Doug Madory confirmed the route hijack and noted RPKI origin validation limited its spread, though FLAG Telecom (AS15412) failed to filter the invalid announcement; operators should reject unauthorized prefixes from AS18101.

title: Exclusive: Daylight gives managed detection and response customers searchable telemetry without a SIEM
blurb: Daylight launched Agentic Security Data Lake, letting MDR customers retain years of telemetry and search it via natural language or KQL without running a SIEM.

title: SentinelOne turns Purple AI loose to investigate threats on its own
blurb: SentinelOne opened its Purple AI Agentic Investigations capability to all Singularity Platform customers, adding "zero-click" autonomous threat investigation that triages, reaches a verdict, and can move to contain a threat without an analyst launching it. The feature runs on existing endpoint, identity, cloud, and third-party telemetry, uses a mix of Claude, GPT, and SentinelOne's Ultraviolet models, and ships with an adjustable human-in-the-loop control plus an auditable evidence chain.

title: Unicorn – The Ultimate CPU Emulator
blurb: Unicorn Engine, the lightweight CPU emulator framework widely used for malware analysis and reverse engineering, won Alibaba Cloud's Asian Star 10x10 Award.

title: What’s new in Android 17? Anti-theft tools, scam detection, and parental controls
blurb: Google started rolling out Android 17 to supported Pixel devices, adding a Contact Picker for granular contact sharing, expanded on-device Live Threat Detection for behaviors like SMS forwarding and accessibility-overlay abuse, and a Find Hub "Mark as lost" mode that biometric-locks missing phones. Several protections, including real-time dynamic signal monitoring of malicious app patterns, roll out to other devices in the second half of the year.

title: Jamf announces new native AI Governance control plane for macOS
blurb: Jamf announced AI Governance, an upcoming Jamf Pro capability that gives IT and security teams OS-level visibility and control over generative AI tools running on managed Macs, including local models and developer tooling that network proxies miss. It offers deep tool discovery, granular policy controls across model access, network, and file system, and Model Context Protocol server restrictions, plus a vendor engine that tracks new controls automatically.

title: Oracle’s Second Monthly Security Updates Deliver 245 Patches
blurb: Oracle released its June 2026 monthly Critical Security Patch Update with 245 fixes, roughly 120 rated critical and about 100 remotely exploitable without authentication, with more than 100 patches landing in Fusion Middleware. The advisory includes CVE-2026-35273, a PeopleSoft flaw that ShinyHunters has reportedly exploited against at least 100 organizations in the education sector, though Oracle does not confirm active exploitation.

title: Tenet Security Emerges From Stealth With $6 Million Seed Funding
blurb: Tenet Security emerged from stealth with $6M in seed funding led by the Westly Group to detect and stop dangerous AI agent behavior at runtime. Founded by Unit 8200 alumni Barak Sternberg and Nevo Poran, who previously built Cisco's AI Defense research team, the firm uses a lightweight runtime sensor to monitor OS behavior, network and API calls, and agent LLM reasoning, flagging runaway or hijacked agents before harmful actions execute.

title: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Joomla JCE Flaw Allowing PHP Code Execution
blurb: CISA added a maximum-severity Joomla Content Editor flaw (CVE-2026-48907, CVSS 10.0) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog after confirming active exploitation that lets unauthenticated users upload and run arbitrary PHP code. The bug affects JCE versions 1.0.0 through 2.9.99.4, was patched in 2.9.99.5 on June 3, and federal civilian agencies must apply the fix by June 19.

title: Three critical Fortinet sandbox bugs splattered by unknown attackers
blurb: Fortinet disclosed three critical vulnerabilities in its FortiSandbox malware-analysis appliance, all of which unknown attackers are already exploiting in the wild. Enterprise security teams running FortiSandbox should patch immediately, since the flaws sit in a tool meant to detect threats, and active exploitation gives intruders a foothold in the part of the stack defenders rely on to catch them.

title: 3 Recently Patched Fortinet FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities in Hacker Crosshairs
blurb: Exploit intelligence firm Defused reported in-the-wild attacks targeting three recently patched Fortinet FortiSandbox flaws, including CVE-2026-39813, a critical authentication bypass, and CVE-2026-39808, a critical OS command injection bug, both patched in April. Separately, SOCRadar identified more than 30,000 Fortinet firewalls compromised in a self-propagating credential-harvesting campaign dubbed FortiBleed spanning organizations across 190-plus countries.

title: Chrome and Firefox Updated to Patch Critical, High-Severity Vulnerabilities
blurb: Google and Mozilla shipped browser updates patching more than 70 vulnerabilities across Chrome and Firefox, including critical use-after-free memory bugs that could enable remote code execution and sandbox escape. Chrome 149 fixes 33 defects and Firefox 152 fixes 40, with Mozilla also updating Firefox ESR, Thunderbird, and Firefox for iOS, making this a broad patch cycle for IT teams managing fleet browser deployments.

title: AMD silently removes memory encryption from consumer Ryzen CPUs, leaving users unaware that they may be vulnerable — security feature vanishes after newer AGESA firmware, AMD engineers go radio silent when pressed about the change
blurb: AMD quietly removed Transparent Secure Memory Encryption (TSME) from consumer Ryzen CPUs after the AGESA 1.2.7.0 firmware update, reserving the memory-encryption feature for its Pro lineup without notifying users. The removal is undetectable on Windows and leaves non-Pro chips exposed to physical memory attacks, pushing buyers who need hardware encryption toward AMD's pricier Pro processors or commercial-tier procurement.

title: CISA orders feds to patch max severity Joomla plugin flaw by Friday
blurb: CISA added a maximum-severity flaw in the Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor plugin (CVE-2026-48907) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and gave federal civilian agencies until Friday to patch under Binding Operational Directive 26-04. The unauthenticated bug allows PHP code execution, is under active automated exploitation with public exploit code, and patching alone does not remove implants from already-compromised installations.

title: AMD's Mext buy shows how AI could solve the RAM shortage it created
blurb: AMD acquired Mext, a memory-optimization startup, betting that smarter software-driven RAM tiering can ease the DRAM scarcity that AI workloads have driven to record prices. For enterprise IT buyers facing inflated server-memory costs and constrained capacity, the deal signals vendors are moving to squeeze more usable memory out of existing silicon rather than waiting on new fab supply to catch up.

title: Windows and Linux users: The deadline to update Secure Boot keys is near
blurb: Three Microsoft-signed Secure Boot certificates expire on June 24, after which firmware and OS components verified against them will no longer chain to a trusted root unless administrators provision the updated keys. IT teams that miss the window risk boot failures or Secure Boot fallback on Windows and Linux fleets, reopening the door to UEFI bootkits that load before the OS and survive reinstalls.

title: Cloudflare DMARC Management is now generally available
blurb: Cloudflare made DMARC Management generally available, offering a redesigned dashboard that gives every customer a unified view of their email authentication posture and a guided path to full DMARC enforcement at no cost. The free tooling parses XML aggregate reports, surfaces SPF and DKIM gaps, and lets IT teams move toward reject policies without hiring an email security consultant.

title: Tesco is sprinting to quit VMware and Broadcom despite rapid migration risks
blurb: Tesco is accelerating its exit from VMware and Broadcom, pushing an aggressive migration timeline despite the operational risk of moving production workloads quickly off the virtualization stack. The retailer's sprint reflects broad enterprise backlash against Broadcom's post-acquisition VMware licensing changes, which bundled products and raised costs, pushing large customers toward alternatives like Nutanix, Proxmox, and KVM-based platforms.

title: Intel’s performance-enhanced 18A-P process enters risk production — drop-in 18A upgrade promises 9% performance improvement at iso-power, cuts thermal resistance by 40%
blurb: Intel’s 18A-P, a drop-in upgrade to 18A promising 9% more performance at iso-power, entered risk production.

title: AMD acquires storage software startup MEXT
blurb: AMD acquired storage software startup MEXT, whose Predictive Memory software makes flash appear as DRAM to the operating system, expanding usable memory capacity while preserving flash economics. The deal pairs MEXT's technology with AMD EPYC processors to cut server memory costs as DRAM prices climb, targeting AI and data center workloads where memory has become a primary performance and cost constraint.

title: Microsoft working on Defender patch for RoguePlanet zero-day
blurb: Microsoft assigned CVE-2026-50656 to the RoguePlanet zero-day and confirmed it is building a Defender patch, a week after researcher Nightmare Eclipse published a proof-of-concept that spawns SYSTEM-level command prompts on fully patched Windows 10 and 11 via a Malware Protection Engine race condition. The flaw works whether or not real-time protection is enabled, leaving enterprise endpoints exposed in the gap until Microsoft ships a fix.

title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises +30K Fortinet Devices
blurb: A Russian-speaking threat group has compromised more than 30,791 internet-facing Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways across 194 countries, harvesting verified working credentials rather than exploiting any Fortinet flaw, according to SOCRadar. Telecommunications, government, and critical-infrastructure organizations dominate the victim list, and over 20% of affected devices belong to enterprises with more than $1B in annual revenue, putting weak default and long-lived admin accounts squarely in scope for credential audits.

title: Microsoft confirms Office apps launch issues after June updates
blurb: Microsoft confirmed that Windows updates released on or after June 9, 2026 break OLE automation, preventing third-party applications like CCH Engagement, Zotero, and dental software Dentrix from launching Office apps or opening Word, Excel, and PowerPoint documents. No fix is available yet, so Microsoft is directing affected enterprises to open documents directly or contact Support for Business for an org-wide workaround.

title: Rokarolla Android trojan targets banking and crypto users, enables device takeover
blurb: Zimperium researchers disclosed Rokarolla, an Android banking trojan that targets 217 banking and cryptocurrency apps and can execute 137 commands on infected devices, including credential harvesting, SMS interception, and full device takeover. Distributed through fake TikTok and Chrome download sites and a dropper posing as Google Play Protect, it abuses Accessibility Services to display phishing overlays and disable Play Protect, raising the stakes for enterprise mobile-device management and BYOD security controls.

title: 1Password Acquires Apono in Reported $250M-$300M Deal
blurb: 1Password acquired Apono, an Israeli just-in-time access governance vendor, in a deal Calcalist reports at $250M to $300M. The buy adds privileged access management for humans, machines, and AI agents to 1Password's identity platform, extending it from credential storage into the access-broker layer alongside its existing SaaS access and device-trust tooling.

title: Salesforce to buy AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6 billion to boost agentic offerings
blurb: Salesforce agreed to acquire AI customer service platform Fin, formerly Intercom, for about $3.6B to extend its Agentforce lineup, with the deal expected to close in Q4 of fiscal 2027. Fin brings an AI agent that resolves chat, email, WhatsApp, SMS, phone, and Slack queries on its proprietary Apex model, giving Salesforce more agentic deployment options as it defends its SaaS stack against AI-native rivals.

title: AI Use by the US Government
blurb: The Office of Management and Budget disclosed 3,611 active or planned federal AI use cases, a 70% jump from the final Biden-era inventory, spanning grant screening at HHS via Palantir, inmate risk scoring at the Bureau of Prisons, and reactor control testing at the Department of Energy. The scale signals expanding federal demand for AI vendors and integrators, alongside procurement and oversight questions as agencies push automated decision-making into high-stakes domains.

title: Brit competition cops order Google to make search rankings less mysterious
blurb: The UK's Competition and Markets Authority ordered Google to disclose how it ranks search results and to give businesses clearer notice before ranking changes, using new powers under its Strategic Market Status designation for the search giant. The transparency mandate gives enterprises and publishers a basis to contest ranking shifts that affect traffic, and signals the CMA will police Google's search dominance more aggressively than past voluntary commitments allowed.

title: Control whether your users can have temporary chats and delete conversations in the Gemini app
blurb: Google added admin controls for temporary chats and conversation deletion in the Gemini app, both ON by default and configurable at the domain, OU, or group level for all Workspace customers. The temporary-chat option lets users hold conversations that bypass history, while deletion controls give admins a governance lever alongside Vault's new retention rules and litigation holds for Gemini.

title: France's digital sovereignty push is struggling to escape the Microsoft gravity well
blurb: France's digital sovereignty effort is hitting a wall on the application layer, where a Nextcloud rollout has migrated storage off Microsoft but failed to move users off Office. The lesson for IT leaders is that swapping infrastructure is the easy part; replacing entrenched productivity software means fighting user habit, file-format lock-in, and feature parity gaps that open-source alternatives still struggle to close.

title: AMD acquires MEXT to add predictive memory optimization to its AI stack
blurb: AMD acquired memory-optimization startup MEXT, adding software that uses predictive algorithms to shuttle hot data between flash and DRAM and make flash behave more like memory. The deal targets a sharp enterprise pain point: Gartner forecasts a 130% rise in combined DRAM and SSD prices by end of 2026, pushing IT buyers toward software that extends effective memory capacity without proportional hardware spend.

title: iRhythm Confirms Data Stolen in Hack
blurb: iRhythm confirmed in a Monday SEC filing that attackers used social engineering to access data on third-party-hosted business applications, with a threat actor claiming to have stolen proprietary data and patient protected health information and demanding a ransom. The company said its clinical, medical device, manufacturing, and financial reporting systems were unaffected, but it has not yet determined how many individuals or what data the breach touched.

title: From RAG to ontology: Databricks bets on context as the key to trusted AI agents
blurb: Databricks introduced Genie Ontology, a preview context layer that extracts business definitions from enterprise data, dashboards, and pipelines into a graph that AI agents query, using a PageRank-style system to rank which source an agent should trust. The pitch to CIOs is consistency and lineage that RAG and vector search can't provide, but analysts say data quality and governance will determine whether the tool actually improves trust enough to drive agent adoption.

title: Windows update leaves third-party Office document launches in limbo
blurb: A recent Windows update broke the ability of third-party applications to launch Office document types, leaving non-Microsoft document handlers in an inconsistent state. IT teams running mixed productivity stacks face help-desk tickets and broken file associations until Microsoft ships a fix, reinforcing the operational cost of managing apps outside the native Microsoft 365 ecosystem.

title: Microsoft might put China’s DeepSeek inside Copilot to tame its AI bills
blurb: Microsoft is exploring a self-hosted, fine-tuned version of DeepSeek V4 to power Copilot Cowork in Microsoft 365, offering it as a cheaper, Azure-hosted alternative to the Anthropic and OpenAI models the agentic assistant runs on today. The move pairs with a shift to usage-based pricing for Cowork and signals a multi-model strategy that hedges Microsoft's dependence on any single lab, though using a Chinese model carries clear political risk as Washington weighs a DeepSeek ban.

title: India's Telegram ban hit the UAE too. Here's how to get around it
blurb: India invoked Section 69A of the IT Act to block Telegram nationwide until June 22 after the platform was used to sell leaked exam materials, and the enforcement spilled across borders when Reliance's AS18101 hijacked Telegram's BGP routes and knocked out access as far as the UAE. Network analysts at Kentik confirmed the hijack, and Telegram has moved the Delhi High Court to challenge a ban that digital rights groups call constitutionally incompatible.

title: Intel starts cooking up enhanced 18A-P silicon for would-be foundry customers
blurb: Intel began producing test silicon on its enhanced 18A-P process, a higher-performance variant of 18A aimed at the external foundry customers it has struggled to win. Landing major chip designers on 18A-P is central to Intel's pitch that its foundry business can compete with TSMC, and early customer silicon is the proof point investors and prospective partners have been waiting for.

title: Chinese memory brands ditch Samsung and Micron for homegrown CXMT and YMTC silicon — Corsair, HP, and Dell are already adopting the China-produced DDR5 chips
blurb: Chinese memory brands including Gloway and KingBank are switching from Samsung, Micron, and SK hynix to domestic DDR5 silicon from CXMT and YMTC, and Corsair, HP, and Dell have already begun qualifying or shipping CXMT-based kits. Backed by government subsidies that prioritize local supply over margin, the shift gives enterprise buyers a memory source insulated from the Big Three's AI-driven pricing premiums.

title: HPE Discover: Neri outlines an AI architecture built for agents
blurb: At HPE Discover 2026, CEO Antonio Neri detailed an agent-oriented AI architecture spanning the full stack, including Private Cloud AI scaling to 256 GPUs, the Juniper-derived networking refresh, and the MCP-native Alletra MPX 10,000 storage layer. The pitch adds agentic governance controls like zero-code agent registration, a three-tier identity model, and Zerto rollback, positioning HPE's Juniper-integrated portfolio against Dell and Nutanix for enterprise agent infrastructure.

title: SentinelOne turns Purple AI loose to investigate threats on its own
blurb: SentinelOne opened its Purple AI Agentic Investigations capability to all Singularity Platform customers, adding zero-click autonomous threat investigation that detects, triages, and moves to contain threats without an analyst launching it. The feature runs on existing endpoint, identity, and cloud telemetry with adjustable human-in-the-loop controls, and arrives alongside Singularity Credits, a unified currency customers draw down for AI work across the platform.

title: Google releases Android 17 for Pixel phones
blurb: Google released the stable build of Android 17, rolling out to Pixel 6 through Pixel 10 devices with features including Bubbles floating-app windows, Screen Reactions, and security upgrades like temporary location permissions and capped PIN guessing. Gemini Intelligence ships later this summer to select advanced devices, and other OEMs including Samsung, with One UI 9, are expected to announce Android 17-based updates in the coming weeks.

title: Will Matter finally be able to do what it should have always done?
blurb: The Connectivity Standards Alliance introduced Matter 1.6 at its Unify conference, headlined by Joint Fabric, a feature that lets a single shared smart-home network be managed by multiple ecosystems so a device added once is controllable by Apple Home, Google Home, and Amazon Alexa without per-app sharing. The spec also adds NFC tap-to-pair, pre-power pairing, and standardized Thermostat Suggestions, addressing the cross-platform multi-admin gaps that have undercut Matter since launch.

title: Andera, which uses AI to automate corporate audit and compliance testing, raised a $37M Series A led by Lightspeed (Ryan Lawler/Axios)
blurb: Andera raised a $37M Series A led by Lightspeed to expand its platform that uses AI to automate corporate audit and compliance testing. The funding targets a control-testing workload that enterprises still handle largely through manual review and Big Four audit fees, positioning Andera against incumbent GRC vendors like AuditBoard and ServiceNow.

title: Meet the new Google Home Speaker, built for Gemini
blurb: Google released the Google Home Speaker, its first audio device built for Gemini, available for pre-order at $99.99 and shipping June 25. The Gemini for Home assistant handles multi-step commands, mid-sentence corrections, and conversational follow-ups, extending Google's Gemini footprint into the home-audio hardware that competes with Amazon Echo and Apple HomePod.

title: Microsoft’s talks to lease Oracle cloud capacity have collapsed, report says
blurb: Microsoft's talks to lease more than $3B in cloud capacity from Oracle have collapsed because Oracle's public cloud lacks FedRAMP certification and the company was unwilling to add it, Business Insider reported, though Oracle disputes the account. The sticking point was compliance rather than price or capacity, since without FedRAMP the leased capacity could not run the government-adjacent workloads that justified the deal for Microsoft.

title: What’s new in Android 17? Anti-theft tools, scam detection, and parental controls
blurb: Google began rolling out Android 17 to supported Pixel devices, adding anti-theft tools, on-device scam and threat detection, a granular Contact Picker, and parental controls, with broader device support arriving later this year. For IT teams managing Android fleets, the new Advanced Protection hardening, dynamic signal monitoring, and tighter accessibility-permission controls give endpoint and MDM policies more built-in defenses against social-engineering and overlay-based attacks.

title: Jamf announces new native AI Governance control plane for macOS
blurb: Jamf announced AI Governance, a capability in Jamf Pro that gives IT and security teams OS-level visibility and policy control over generative AI tools running on managed Macs, including local models, developer tools, and Model Context Protocol servers. The native approach closes a gap network proxies and cross-platform endpoint tools miss on Apple Silicon, where AI apps run as background processes, and arrives as Gartner projects AI governance spending to pass $1B by 2030.

title: UK.gov links up with LinkedIn for jobs market intel from 40M accounts
blurb: The UK government partnered with LinkedIn to tap labor-market data from 40M UK accounts, feeding real-time skills and hiring signals into its workforce and economic policy planning. The arrangement gives Microsoft's LinkedIn a public-sector data channel and raises questions about reliance on a single commercial platform for official jobs intelligence.

title: Who owns the control plane? Google Cloud Next 2026 and the real contest in agentic AI
blurb: The author argues the real enterprise AI contest has shifted from model selection to ownership of the agentic control plane, the semantic context layer that decides what agents know, what they can do, and who is accountable. The strategic warning is lock-in: Gartner expects 40% of enterprise apps to embed agents by end of 2026 but over 40% of agentic projects to be canceled by 2027, so enterprises that don't own their own ontology cede governance to whichever vendor controls that layer.

title: HPE steers more Juniper Networks self-driving tech into its stack
blurb: HPE expanded its Juniper-derived self-driving networking across its portfolio, extending agentic AIOps from Mist and Marvis AI to wired CX switches, Aruba Central, and data center operations with predictive maintenance. New QFX5140 and QFX5252 switches target inference clusters and AMD's Helios rack, while Mist Data Center Assurance is now available through GreenLake alongside a unified AI-native SASE update.

title: A modest proposal: Reformat everything to make documents more palatable to AI
blurb: The author argues that enterprises should restructure documents into AI-friendly formats rather than expecting models to wrestle with the messy layouts humans produce, floating a standardized "DocLang" as the fix. The implication for IT leaders is a familiar trade-off: cleaner machine ingestion and better RAG accuracy on one side, a fresh content-conversion mandate and yet another format standard to govern on the other.

title: Tether is shipping TurboQuant KV-cache quantization with Vulkan support into its QVAC SDK
blurb: Tether shipped TurboQuant KV-cache quantization with Vulkan support in version 0.12.0 of its QVAC SDK, cutting VRAM use by up to 5x with negligible precision loss regardless of context length. Tether is the first team to bring Google's KV-cache compression to a publicly available local model, making long-context inference viable on user-grade devices where an 8GB cache previously exceeded available memory.

title: Gemini in Chrome expands to more languages and regions, including Latin America, Africa, and the Middle East
blurb: Google expanded Gemini in Chrome to more languages and regions, including Latin America, Africa, and the Middle East, bringing tab summarization, content generation, and Gemini Live voice chat to all Workspace plans on ChromeOS, macOS, and Windows. The feature is on by default for eligible domains, so IT admins must opt out at the domain, OU, or group level if they want to gate browser-native AI access.

title: Oracle’s Second Monthly Security Updates Deliver 245 Patches
blurb: Oracle released its June 2026 monthly Critical Security Patch Update with 245 new patches, roughly 120 rated critical and about 100 remotely exploitable without authentication, with Fusion Middleware accounting for more than 100 fixes. The advisory addresses PeopleSoft flaw CVE-2026-35273, which security firms tie to ShinyHunters attacks on at least 100 organizations, though Oracle stops short of confirming active exploitation.

title: 5 things CIOs must do as sovereignty becomes a design constraint
blurb: Geographic location has shifted from a deployment detail to a core architectural constraint, as CIOs reweight sourcing decisions around geopolitical risk, AI regulation, and data residency rather than total cost of ownership. The piece argues enterprises aren't abandoning hyperscalers but are redesigning how they use them, treating sovereign cloud options and cross-border data flows as first-order design variables. The practical takeaway: build a regional regulatory map into architecture reviews before consolidating further on any single global platform.

title: Blackstone-Led Creditors Take Over Ailing Software Firm Medallia
blurb: A Blackstone-led group of creditors is taking control of Medallia after Thoma Bravo, the experience-management vendor's owner, declined to inject fresh capital into the struggling SaaS firm. The handover hands lenders the keys to a company Thoma Bravo took private for $6.4B in 2021, and it signals stress in the highly leveraged buyouts that fueled enterprise-software dealmaking over the past five years.

title: Orca deploys quantum computer to major Japanese enterprise customer
blurb: Orca Computing deployed one of its PT-2 photonic quantum systems to an unnamed major Japanese industrial customer, marking the first commercial installation of its hardware in an enterprise facility rather than a research setting. Completed in under a week with partner Toyota Tsusho, the system integrates into the customer's existing cloud infrastructure for hybrid quantum-AI workloads and will later upgrade to Orca's PT-3 platform, expected to ship for benchmarking in August.

title: Flash pricing — the elephant in the room for enterprise storage
blurb: The author argues that the decade-long vendor narrative of flash reaching pricing parity with HDDs was never true, and AI workloads have exposed it. Large AI datasets, object storage, and pre-compressed data deliver far lower data-reduction ratios than the "effective capacity" math assumed, while hyperscaler NAND buying squeezes SSD supply. Everpure's (formerly Pure Storage) CEO citing a third supply-chain disruption to justify price hikes signals enterprises should keep cheap HDDs in the mix rather than commit all-flash.

title: Amazon S3 annotations: attach rich, queryable context directly to your objects
blurb: AWS announced S3 annotations, letting teams attach up to 1,000 named annotations per object (1 MB each, 1 GB total) in JSON, XML, YAML, or plain text, editable without rewriting the object. Annotations travel with objects through copy, replication, and cross-region transfers, and flow into S3 Metadata tables queryable from Athena, giving agentic workflows scalable context without expensive retrieval.

title: AWS WAF adds AI traffic monetization capability to help content owners charge AI bots for content access
blurb: AWS WAF added AI traffic monetization, a Bot Control capability that lets content providers and publishers price, meter, and collect payment from AI bots and agents hitting their content and APIs. Owners set a price, accept payment through third-party providers, and grant scoped access directly at the edge, turning unwanted crawler traffic into a revenue stream without standing up separate gateway infrastructure.

title: Cloudflare DMARC Management is now generally available
blurb: Cloudflare made DMARC Management generally available to all customers for free, shipping a redesigned dashboard that gives a unified view of email authentication posture and guides domains toward full DMARC enforcement without parsing XML reports by hand. The tool ties together SPF, DKIM, and DMARC reporting to surface who is spoofing your domain and block impersonation before mail reaches inboxes.

title: What’s new with Terraform + Ansible
blurb: HashiCorp released Terraform Ansible collection 2.0, powered by pyTFE, giving teams an API-first way to manage Terraform workspaces, runs, remote execution, and outputs directly from Ansible playbooks under role-based access controls. The collection eliminates the custom scripts and brittle glue code teams previously maintained to keep the two tools in sync, cutting maintenance overhead and reducing the upgrade breakage that disconnected permission models caused.

title: Introducing tfctl: The CLI for HCP Terraform and TFE
blurb: HashiCorp released tfctl, the first official CLI for HCP Terraform and Terraform Enterprise platform operations like canceling runs, creating workspaces, and managing variables that the Terraform CLI doesn't cover. Built on the OpenAPI spec for full API coverage, it ships with dry-run guardrails, schema discovery, and JSON, markdown, or table output, and is scoped so delete commands require interactive confirmation to keep autonomous agents from running them.

title: Tesco is sprinting to quit VMware and Broadcom despite rapid migration risks
blurb: Tesco is accelerating its exit from VMware and Broadcom, moving production workloads off the stack faster than typical migration timelines to escape steep post-acquisition licensing costs. The compressed schedule raises blast-radius and rollback concerns, since rapid hypervisor migration at retail scale leaves little room to validate storage, networking, and failover before cutover.

title: Implementing workload identity with HashiCorp Vault and SPIFFE
blurb: HashiCorp published a guide to using Vault as a SPIFFE identity issuer and broker, issuing SVIDs to workloads so services authenticate by verified identity rather than long-lived secrets. The post draws a clear boundary for where SPIRE belongs, with Vault handling issuance and authorization brokering while SPIRE manages node and workload attestation. The payoff is fewer static credentials to rotate and a consistent identity layer across clusters.

title: Report: Microsoft to use AWS to help GitHub deal with a major surge in demand
blurb: GitHub is tapping AWS for short-term capacity to absorb a 30x demand surge driven by agentic coding tools, after an October 2025 plan to scale 10x proved insufficient and triggered several small outages. The move is a stopgap multi-cloud play while Microsoft continues its longer migration to fully host GitHub on Azure by 2027.

title: Hetzner Price Adjustment
blurb: Hetzner raised prices across its cloud servers, dedicated hardware, and storage products, citing higher energy, hardware, and infrastructure costs as the drivers behind the adjustment. The increase narrows the cost gap that has made Hetzner a default budget alternative to AWS and GCP for teams running their own k8s and CI fleets, and the 574-comment HN thread reflects how sensitive that pricing has been to infra-team build-versus-rent math.

title: What job interviews taught me about Kubernetes
blurb: The author uses Kubernetes interview questions as a lens for the internals practitioners gloss over in daily operations, walking through how the scheduler, kubelet, and control loops actually behave under questioning. The piece argues that interview prep exposes the gap between running kubectl commands and understanding why a pod is stuck pending or a node goes NotReady. Useful as a self-audit for platform engineers who operate clusters without having traced the reconciliation path end to end.

title: 10Gb/s Ethernet: switching to a Broadcom SFP+ module
blurb: A 10GBASE-T SFP+ module in the author's study switch was overheating to 93C and thermal-flapping, shutting down at 95C and dropping the LAN until air conditioning kept it cool. The fix was swapping the Marvell-based MikroTik S+RJ10 for a Broadcom BCM84891-based 10Gtek module, which draws roughly 1.6W versus the older generation's higher power draw. The takeaway for anyone running 10GBASE-T over copper: check the PHY chip, since the Broadcom generation runs cool enough to avoid 24/7 cooling as a workaround.

title: Intel-born networking tech resurfaces as InfiniBand alternative for DoE supers
blurb: Cornelis Networks is positioning Omni-Path, the high-speed interconnect Intel originally developed and spun out, as an Ethernet- and InfiniBand-competitive fabric for Department of Energy supercomputers chasing lower-latency collective operations at exascale. The pitch targets HPC and AI clusters where InfiniBand's Nvidia ownership and Ethernet's congestion behavior leave operators wanting a third option for predictable tail latency on tightly coupled jobs.

title: The Chainguard Athena coalition already shipped 2,000 patches across 500 open source projects
blurb: Chainguard launched Athena, an industry coalition that pools pre-disclosure vulnerability findings and remediates them under embargo, going live with more than two dozen members including Cisco, Cloudflare, Docker, JPMorganChase, and PwC. The pipeline has already processed 20,000 findings and shipped 2,000 patches across 500 open source projects, many surfaced by frontier AI programs like Anthropic's Project Glasswing and OpenAI's Daybreak, with metadata published as an OSV feed.

title: HPE Discover: Neri outlines an AI architecture built for agents
blurb: At HPE Discover 2026, CEO Antonio Neri detailed an AI infrastructure stack rebuilt for agentic workloads, including Private Cloud AI scaling to 256 GPUs, the Alletra MPX 10,000 storage layer with native MCP support, and Juniper-based networking from GPU racks to the inference edge. The agentic operations tier adds zero-code agent registration across any framework, a three-tier identity model, and Zerto clean-state rollback for when agents err.

title: Why cloud native belongs at the heart of agentic AI: Lessons from building a multi-agent security platform on Kubernetes
blurb: An engineer at Orange Innovation documents building an internal real-time security-operations platform where each AI agent runs as its own Kubernetes Deployment, not an in-process module, giving every agent independent resource limits, identity, and restart policy. The stack pairs Falco with eBPF and an Isolation Forest model to pre-filter syscall events before they reach LangGraph-driven agents coordinated over A2A. Treating agents as microservices means canary rollouts, HPA, and namespace isolation apply without reinvention.

title: Meta launches AI Mode in Facebook search to answer questions
blurb: Meta launched AI Mode in Facebook Search, which uses Meta AI to answer questions directly with responses grounded in public posts, Groups, and Reels rather than a standard list of results. Meta hasn't said how AI Mode selects which content surfaces or whether brands and creators can see when their posts are used, leaving discovery and attribution on Facebook an open question for marketers.

title: TikTok Shop Bans AI Voices, Prerecorded Audio From Promotional Livestreams
blurb: TikTok Shop banned AI-generated voices, prerecorded audio, slideshows, and static-image content from promotional livestreams and shoppable videos, requiring all verbal communication to happen in real time and creators to appear on camera with the physical product. The rules land as eMarketer projects TikTok Shop will hit $23.4B in US sales in 2026, and as 31% of consumers say AI-generated marketing makes them trust a brand less versus 7% who trust it more.

title: Adobe brings GEO to CX Enterprise with Adobe Brand Visibility
blurb: Adobe launched Adobe Brand Visibility, a generative engine optimization product inside Adobe CX Enterprise that draws on nearly 300M real-world AI search prompts to show brands where they win or lose citations across ChatGPT, Google AI Mode, Microsoft Copilot, and Perplexity. The release, Adobe's first GEO tool since its May Semrush acquisition, lands as AI traffic to US retail sites jumped 1,324% between October 2024 and May 2026.

title: Pinterest launches an experimental AI shopping app called ‘Ask Pinterest’
blurb: Pinterest launched Ask Pinterest, a limited-access experimental app that turns its Taste Graph into a conversational shopping interface for multi-step queries like planning a dinner party or furnishing a room. The company also introduced a Pinterest MCP and new AI ad tools for advertisers, positioning itself against Google, ChatGPT, and Meta in agentic shopping while keeping discovery on its own first-party data rather than licensing it out.

title: Brit competition cops order Google to make search rankings less mysterious
blurb: The UK's Competition and Markets Authority ordered Google to give businesses clearer information about how its search rankings work, using new powers that designate Google a holder of strategic market status. The ruling means SEO and content teams may finally get documented signals on what drives placement, reducing reliance on reverse-engineering and third-party guesswork to defend organic traffic.

title: Amazon could face billions in fines as the FTC eyes its ad business
blurb: The FTC has drafted a potential complaint accusing Amazon of misleading advertisers over hidden reserve pricing in its sponsored listings, a case that could carry billions in civil penalties. The real exposure comes from state attorneys general, whose consumer-protection laws allow fines per violation per day, multiplied across an ad business that brought Amazon $68.6B in 2025.

title: Amazon Launches Program for Independent Creators Navigating AI Search
blurb: Amazon launched Amazon Content Partners, a gated-preview program that lets independent creators monitor, block, rate-limit, or monetize AI crawler traffic through AWS WAF while adding a 1% Associates commission boost, premium ad access, and $100 monthly in AWS credits. The pitch lands as discovery fragments toward AI and social search, with one Adobe Express survey finding 49% of consumers now use TikTok as a search engine.

title: How Stripe, Google, Canva, Cloudflare and Higgsfield Are Actually Selling in 2026
blurb: Across six SaaStr AI 2026 GTM sessions, Stripe, Google, Canva, Cloudflare, Owner, and Higgsfield independently converged on the same four patterns: build and sell in parallel, centralize AI intelligence, treat agents as a real customer, and charge for outcomes rather than seats. Stripe's data shows top AI companies grew 120% in 2025 and 175% in 2026, with Lovable hitting $100M in revenue in eight months and Cursor reaching a $1B run rate in under two years. The shared takeaway for B2B founders is that outcome-based pricing and agent-aware GTM are becoming table stakes.

title: LinkedIn will tell others how you really use Adobe’s apps
blurb: LinkedIn launched "connected apps," a feature that auto-generates verified descriptions of how members actually use linked tools, with HubSpot, Buffer, Fiverr, and 14 others live now and Adobe Express, Gong, and Copy.AI coming soon. The statements are AI-generated, can't be edited by the user, and update in real time, giving recruiters tool-specific proof of proficiency rather than a self-reported skills list.

title: X Enhances Ads Manager With Unified Conversion Tracking, Live Diagnostics
blurb: X rolled out three new Ads Manager features for all advertisers: a no-code Google Tag Manager integration for Pixel and Conversion API setup, a consolidated CAPI hub in Events Manager, and a real-time dashboard that surfaces Pixel and CAPI event health as issues arise. The updates lower the technical bar for conversion tracking on X, part of what the company calls the largest rebuild of its ad platform in 20 years.

title: Topics matter for third-party authority signals
blurb: AI search engines rebuild their trusted-source set for every topic, so off-property authority building has to be topic-specific rather than generic digital PR. In one sample, competitor domains accounted for 33.5% of AI citations on invoicing queries but only 7% on starting-a-business queries, meaning the source types AI reaches for nearly flip between subjects. Audit the exact publishers, research, and expert sources AI cites for your target topic, then aim links and mentions there instead of borrowing a PR plan from a neighboring vertical.

title: Substack Expands Native Creator Partnership Program, Taps Former Roku Exec to Lead Sponsorships Push
blurb: Substack launched the next phase of its native sponsorships program, hiring former Roku and PayPal exec Dan Robbins as its first Head of Brand Sponsorships and signing inaugural partners including Uber, Balenciaga, T-Mobile, and Yahoo Scout that are collectively investing millions in creators. The platform handles brand matchmaking and logistics while creators keep editorial control, positioning sponsorships as a complement to subscriptions, which already pay out across more than 100,000 publishers.

title: Podcast Audiences Are Buying Into the Host … Brands May Need to Do the Same
blurb: Edison Research's "The Podcast Consumer 2026" found monthly listening peaks among adults 35-54 at 68%, ahead of the 64% for ages 12-34, and 66% of weekly listeners now consume podcasts at home. The report's takeaway for advertisers is that host trust, not just show reach or category fit, drives purchase influence and ad effectiveness. Marketers buying placements on audience size alone are underpricing the host-listener relationship that actually moves loyalty and conversions.

title: The 3-Billion-Impression Campaign That Kept Its Brand Name Hidden Until the Credits
blurb: e.l.f. Beauty and agency Movers+Shakers turned a TikTok pain point, fighting over vanity space, into "Vanity Vandals," a nine-minute true-crime mockumentary that ran most of its length without showing the brand name and still drove 3B press impressions, 40M social views, and 92% positive sentiment. The play is entertainment-first branding sourced from social listening: hashtags like #MakeupMess (115M+ views) confirmed the behavior was real before production started, so the creative felt native to the conversation rather than an ad interrupting it.

title: A comparison of human and LLM-simulated participants in a writing style task
blurb: A study tested whether GPT-4o prompted as a synthetic participant could replace 30 humans in evaluating how writing-style preference algorithms adapt over repeated interactions. The LLM showed bias and a lack of depth in both its text generation and judgment, preventing it from accurately reproducing human behavior, while the humans revealed their own biases. For PMs tempted to run user research on simulated participants, the takeaway is that LLM stand-ins miss the human factors that make a study worth running.

title: The autonomy dial: a pattern toolkit for designing human control over AI
blurb: This essay offers a "autonomy dial" framework for deciding how much an AI feature acts on its own, paired with six reusable patterns for keeping humans in control. For PMs shipping AI features, it turns a vague "how much should we automate" debate into a concrete spec decision, with the control patterns serving as a checklist for where oversight, confirmation, or reversibility need to be designed in.

title: LinkedIn will tell others how you really use Adobe’s apps
blurb: LinkedIn launched a "connected apps" feature that auto-generates verified, non-editable descriptions of how members actually use linked tools like HubSpot, Fiverr, and Buffer, with Adobe Express, GitHub Copilot, and Zapier coming soon. The statements update in real time without user approval, giving recruiters tool-specific proof of proficiency and giving PMs a new signal to weigh when hiring for roles that require hands-on fluency with particular software.

title: How to design AI agent loops: schedules, goals, and subagents in Claude Code and Codex
blurb: The author breaks down four loop types for AI coding agents — heartbeat, cron, hook, and goal — and argues a loop is just an automated prompt, then names the five things any production loop needs: work trees, skills, plugins, subagents, and state tracking. He builds two live examples: a daily aging-PR reviewer in Claude Code that self-schedules and spawns subagents, and a weekly skills-identification loop in Codex whose goal-based subagents validate their own output. The practical warning for PMs: goal loops are the hardest to write and the fastest way to burn tokens for nothing.

title: Pinterest launches an experimental AI shopping app called ‘Ask Pinterest’
blurb: Pinterest released Ask Pinterest, a limited-access experimental standalone app that turns its visual discovery into a conversational, chatbot-style interface for multi-step shopping queries like planning a dinner party or furnishing a room over time. The app runs on Pinterest's Taste Graph and users' saved Pins to personalize recommendations, letting the company test agentic shopping without disrupting the main app and feed what it learns back into the flagship.

title: Coinbase unveils ‘System Update’ unlocking an AI advisor, global unified liquidity, options trading and more
blurb: Coinbase rolled out a "System Update" bundling an SEC-registered AI investment advisor, unified global liquidity across its US and international spot and derivatives platforms, options trading, and thematic and pre-IPO perpetual futures. The release extends Coinbase's "Everything Exchange" push into equities, perps, and prediction markets, mirroring similar expansions by Kraken, OKX, and Binance.

title: Ripple invests in Flutterwave, pushing its stablecoin and XRP Ledger into payments across Africa
blurb: Ripple invested in African payments company Flutterwave's Series E round, valuing the fintech at $3.2 billion and integrating its RLUSD stablecoin and the XRP Ledger into Flutterwave's cross-border payments infrastructure. The deal routes stablecoin settlement over the XRPL across Africa, where remittances and dollar liquidity access remain key constraints, advancing RLUSD as a real-world payments rail rather than a trading instrument.

title: Coinbase intoduces AI advisor, stock options, and pre-IPO markets in finance push
blurb: Coinbase rolled out a broad suite of new products spanning U.S. stock and ETF trading, equity and crypto options, tokenized stocks backed one-for-one by underlying shares, and pre-IPO perpetual futures tied to private firms like SpaceX, alongside an SEC-registered AI advisor and automated trading agents. The expansion positions COIN to compete directly with brokerages, banks, and fintechs as it builds toward an "everything exchange.

title: Binance Could Face EU Exit as Reports Say Greek Regulator is Set to Reject MiCA License
blurb: Greece's Hellenic Capital Market Commission is set to reject Binance's MiCA license application, according to Reuters, putting the world's largest exchange on course to lose EU client access as soon as July 1. Binance disputes the report, saying the HCMC deemed its application compliant and intended to authorize it, and that it is internally drawing up contingency plans for an EU exit.

title: The Ghosts of Polymarket: When Off-Chain Matches Meet On-Chain Reverts
blurb: This paper analyzes "Ghost Fills" on Polymarket, the consistency gap created by its hybrid architecture that matches orders off-chain but settles them on-chain. The authors built GHOSTHUNTER to reconstruct failed settlements from on-chain traces, examining 1,952,440 reverted match-order transactions where attackers exploited the time gap to invalidate already-matched orders before finalization. They identify four attack vectors—nonce bump, balance drain, allowance revoke, and proxy trap—realized across 35 evolving variants.

title: Senate, House reach agreement on housing bill banning CBDC through 2030
blurb: Senate and House leaders reached a bipartisan agreement on the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which attaches a provision barring the Federal Reserve from issuing a CBDC or any substantially similar digital asset through Dec. 31, 2030. The anti-CBDC language, pushed by House Republicans onto must-pass housing legislation, now heads to a first procedural vote in the Senate before returning to the House.

title: Australia’s High Court backs regulator in Block Earner crypto yield case
blurb: Australia's High Court unanimously ruled that Block Earner's now-discontinued crypto yield product required an Australian financial services licence, overturning a 2025 appeal court decision that had cleared the firm. The ruling sets binding precedent that yield-bearing crypto offerings can qualify as financial products, raising the licensing bar for digital asset operators across the Australian market.

title: Zama, Morpho and Steakhouse launch first ‘confidential DeFi yield’ vault on Ethereum
blurb: Zama, Morpho, and Steakhouse Financial launched the first confidential DeFi yield vault on Ethereum, using Zama's fully homomorphic encryption to keep depositor balances and positions encrypted onchain while still accruing yield through Morpho's lending markets. The design targets institutions and treasuries that want onchain returns without exposing holdings to competitors or front-runners watching the public mempool.

title: Heir to 135-year Gulf dynasty is moving a $6 trillion trade market onto blockchain rails
blurb: Abdulla Kanoo, an heir to Bahrain's 135-year-old Kanoo business dynasty, is building ARP Digital, a blockchain settlement layer for cross-border trade among emerging economies that has already processed over $3.5B in volume for 450-plus institutions. Licensed in Bahrain and approved in principle in Dubai, ARP integrates with Fireblocks and is targeting a Global South trade market Kanoo pegs at $6T in 2024 and projected to reach $32T by 2030.

title: U.S. senators urge Treasury not to leave states out of GENIUS Act stablecoin process
blurb: A bipartisan group of senators led by Cynthia Lummis pressed the Treasury to give states an explicit certification process under the GENIUS Act, arguing its proposed principles omitted the timeline and procedural steps states need to prove their stablecoin oversight matches federal standards. Without clear guidance, the lawmakers warned, the certification process could be applied in a way that forecloses state-level participation entirely.

title: New Rokarolla Android malware targets 217 banking, crypto apps
blurb: Researchers at Zimperium identified Rokarolla, a new Android banking trojan that targets 217 banking and cryptocurrency apps using 137 commands, spreading via fake Google Chrome and TikTok download sites that impersonate Google Play Protect. Once installed, it requests Accessibility permissions to take administrative control, log keystrokes, disable Play Protect, and display fake login overlays that harvest credentials, lock-screen PINs, and financial data.

title: Collector Crypt Fees Jump 129% in a Week as Solflare Brings Card-Pack Trading Into the Wallet
blurb: Collector Crypt, the Solana platform that tokenizes graded physical trading cards, saw weekly fee revenue jump 129% to $3.86M after Solflare embedded its randomized card-pack mechanic directly into the wallet. The integration routes Solflare's roughly four million monthly active users into the gacha pack-opening flow, pushing the platform's all-time revenue past $52.8M.

title: Tribal Coalition Files Amicus Briefs to Keep Prediction Markets Off Native Land
blurb: A tribal coalition filed amicus briefs arguing CFTC preemption for Kalshi would void tribal-state gaming compacts on Native land.

title: A new Bittensor proposal would turn validators into something like fund managers
blurb: Bittensor developer "unconst" submitted Root Reborn, a code proposal that would stop validators from automatically selling subnet tokens for TAO each block and instead have them reinvest yield into chosen subnets, building compounding baskets that stakers can still cash out to TAO anytime. The change turns validators into active capital allocators and replaces constant sell pressure with net buying, though it remains a GitHub test-network submission, not a live mainnet change.

title: ‘He had his day in court:’ Sens. Lummis and Gallego press Trump not to pardon former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried
blurb: Senators Cynthia Lummis and Ruben Gallego introduced a Senate resolution urging President Trump not to grant executive clemency to convicted former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried, who is serving a 25-year sentence for defrauding customers, lenders, and investors. The move follows Trump's pardons of other crypto figures including Ross Ulbricht and former Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao over the past year.

title: Forget retail traders: The real multi-trillion-dollar crypto future is building infrastructure for machines
blurb: Singapore-based Stables raised attention for AI-native payment middleware that routes stablecoin transactions across Asia's fragmented cross-border trade corridor, embedding an Anthropic Model Context Protocol server so autonomous software can handle compliance, FX, and settlement. CEO Bernardo Bilotta is betting on machine-to-machine payments over retail volume, targeting an Asia-Pacific B2B e-commerce market projected to top $28.9 trillion this year.

title: DeFi: Why Coinbase and Apollo Are Building on $2B Morpho
blurb: Morpho, the onchain lending protocol built around isolated lending markets, raised $175M at a roughly $2B valuation co-led by Paradigm, a16z crypto, and Ribbit, with Apollo, Circle Ventures, and VanEck participating. The protocol now holds over $11B in deposits and powers credit products for Coinbase, Kraken, Société Générale, and Anchorage, with its market-isolation design drawing interest after Aave absorbed up to $236M in bad debt.

title: Coinbase unveils ‘System Update’ unlocking an AI advisor, global unified liquidity, options trading and more
blurb: Coinbase released a "System Update" bundling an SEC-registered AI investment advisor, unified global liquidity across its U.S. and international spot and derivatives platforms, and new options, thematic-index, and pre-IPO perpetual products. The update extends Coinbase's "Everything Exchange" push to compete with Kraken, OKX, and Binance, adding a 5% bitcoin reward on Coinbase One Card bookings through a new Booking.com-built Travel Portal.

title: Ripple invests in Flutterwave, pushing its stablecoin and XRP Ledger into payments across Africa
blurb: Ripple invested in Flutterwave's Series E round, valuing the African payments company at $3.2B and integrating its RLUSD stablecoin and the XRP Ledger into Flutterwave's cross-border settlement infrastructure. The deal positions stablecoins as a working alternative to correspondent banking for African remittances, where dollar liquidity is scarce and traditional rails are slow and costly.

title: Coinbase intoduces AI advisor, stock options, and pre-IPO markets in finance push
blurb: Coinbase rolled out a broad suite of products spanning stock and ETF trading, equity and crypto options, pre-IPO perpetual futures, tokenized US equities, and an SEC-registered AI advisor, pushing toward its "everything exchange" ambition. The move puts the largest US crypto exchange in direct competition with brokerages, banks, and fintechs, layering consumer offerings like a USDC-backed credit card and borrowing against staked Solana on top.

title: Binance Could Face EU Exit as Reports Say Greek Regulator is Set to Reject MiCA License
blurb: Greece's Hellenic Capital Market Commission is set to reject Binance's MiCA license application, Reuters reported, which would bar the world's largest exchange from serving EU residents as soon as July 1 under MiCA's transitional schedule. Binance disputes the account, saying HCMC found its application compliant and signaled intent to authorize, and warns a rejected passporting path would push liquidity and tax revenue outside the bloc.

title: Senate, House reach agreement on housing bill banning CBDC through 2030
blurb: Senate and House leaders reached a bipartisan agreement on the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which bars the Federal Reserve from issuing a CBDC or any substantially similar digital asset through December 31, 2030. House Republicans attached the anti-CBDC provision to the must-pass housing bill, aligning with the Trump administration's stance that a US central bank digital currency is off the table.

title: Build Your Own Transaction Foundation Model for Financial Intelligence
blurb: NVIDIA published a developer walkthrough for building a transaction foundation model end-to-end, using transformer pretraining on unlabeled transaction sequences to replace brittle hand-engineered features across fraud detection, credit scoring, and segmentation. The example reproduces a near-50% lift in average precision over a strong XGBoost baseline on the IBM TabFormer fraud dataset, following the same approach behind Stripe, Nubank, Visa, and Mastercard production models.

title: Australia’s High Court backs regulator in Block Earner crypto yield case
blurb: Australia's High Court unanimously ruled that crypto firm Block Earner required an Australian financial services licence to offer its yield-bearing product, overturning a 2025 appeal decision that had cleared the company. The ruling gives ASIC firmer footing to treat crypto yield offerings as regulated financial products, raising the licensing bar for any digital-asset firm operating in the Australian market.

title: Zama, Morpho and Steakhouse launch first ‘confidential DeFi yield’ vault on Ethereum
blurb: Zama, Morpho and Steakhouse launched the first confidential DeFi yield vault on Ethereum, using Zama's fully homomorphic encryption to let institutions earn yield without exposing balances or positions on-chain. The setup targets firms that want DeFi returns but can't operate with public ledgers, addressing a core barrier to institutional on-chain treasury deployment.

title: BlackRock Launches BITA, a Covered-Call Bitcoin ETF Designed to Generate Monthly Income
blurb: BlackRock listed the iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF (BITA) on Nasdaq, writing monthly call options on 25% to 35% of NAV over its $51B IBIT spot fund to pass option premiums to investors as monthly income. The 0.65% structure caps upside on the hedged portion in exchange for yield, applying JEPI-style covered-call mechanics to bitcoin against rivals like YieldMax and Roundhill.

title: Payments startup Flutterwave hits $3.2B valuation, backed by Ripple
blurb: Flutterwave raised a Series E that values the African payments infrastructure company at $3.2B, with Ripple taking an equity stake to jointly expand digital-asset and cross-border services across the continent. The deal builds on Flutterwave's push to unify fragmented African rails, following its Mono acquisition and a Polygon-backed stablecoin product that lets businesses bypass slow correspondent banking routed through London.

title: New Rokarolla Android malware targets 217 banking, crypto apps
blurb: A new Android banking trojan called Rokarolla targets 217 banking and crypto apps with 137 commands, spreading through fake Chrome and TikTok installers that impersonate Google Play Protect, according to Zimperium. Once granted Accessibility permissions, it takes full administrative control of the device, using fake login overlays to harvest credentials, card data, and lock-screen PINs while disabling Play Protect to evade detection.

title: DeFi: Why Coinbase and Apollo Are Building on $2B Morpho
blurb: Morpho raised $175M at a roughly $2B valuation, co-led by Paradigm, a16z crypto, and Ribbit with participation from Apollo, Circle Ventures, and VanEck, as the onchain lending protocol crossed $11B in deposits and a client roster spanning Coinbase, Kraken, and Société Générale. Its isolated-market design gained appeal after Aave absorbed up to $236M in bad debt and $6B in withdrawals, positioning Morpho as the backend infrastructure for institutional on-chain credit.

title: Heir to 135-year Gulf dynasty is moving a $6 trillion trade market onto blockchain rails
blurb: Abdulla Kanoo, an heir to Bahrain's 135-year-old Kanoo business dynasty, is building ARP Digital, a blockchain settlement layer for cross-border trade among emerging economies that has processed over $3.5B in volume for 450-plus institutions. Licensed in Bahrain, approved in principle in Dubai, and integrated with Fireblocks, ARP targets a Global South trade market that topped $6 trillion in 2024 and could reach $32 trillion by 2030.

title: ECB’s Lagarde Warns AI Is Huge Risk for Financial Stability
blurb: ECB President Christine Lagarde said AI poses a significant risk to financial stability, warning the technology could amplify shocks and trigger crises if left unchecked. The comments signal the ECB is weighing supervisory and macroprudential responses as banks and payment firms accelerate AI adoption across credit, trading, and fraud-detection systems.

title: ‘He had his day in court:’ Sens. Lummis and Gallego press Trump not to pardon former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried
blurb: Senators Cynthia Lummis and Ruben Gallego introduced a Senate resolution declaring that Sam Bankman-Fried, the FTX founder serving 25 years for defrauding customers, should under no circumstances receive a presidential pardon. The bipartisan move comes from two lawmakers central to crypto legislation and follows Trump's pardons of Ross Ulbricht and former Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao, signaling unease over executive clemency reshaping crypto-industry accountability.

title: Egypt’s Pound Becomes World’s Top Currency as Oil Shock Reverses
blurb: Egypt's pound became the world's best-performing currency after a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz sent oil prices sharply lower.

title: Bubble Risks Are Rising as AI Borrowing Balloons
blurb: Bloomberg argues that risks of an AI bubble are mounting as the sector's borrowing balloons, with debt loads now diverging sharply in quality across players. The analysis warns that investment-grade hyperscalers can absorb the leverage while weaker AI borrowers face refinancing strain, a split that matters for fintech lenders and credit desks underwriting exposure to the buildout.
